title: Plant and machine operatives n.e.c.
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Design, develop, and evaluate the operation of marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment, such as power supply and propulsion systems. (Based on 151 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Disassemble axles to repair, adjust, and replace defective parts, using hand tools; install and adjust brakes and brake pads; help customers select bicycles that fit their body sizes and intended bicycle uses; sell bicycles and accessories; install new tires and tubes; order bicycle parts; repair holes in tire tubes, using scrapers and patches; shape replacement parts, using bench grinders; clean and lubricate bicycle parts; align wheels; install, repair, and replace equipment or accessories, such as handlebars, stands, lights, and seats; install and adjust speed and gear mechanisms; assemble new bicycles;

Build wheels by cutting and threading new spokes; perform routine equipment maintenance such as cleaning and lubricating machines or replacing broken needles; inspect finished products to ensure that shoes have been completed according to specifications;

Assemble, install, test, or maintain electrical or electronic wiring, equipment, appliances, apparatus, or fixtures, using hand tools or power tools; inspect electrical systems, equipment, or components to identify hazards, defects, or the need for adjustment or repair, and to ensure compliance with codes; connect wires to circuit breakers, transformers, or other components; diagnose malfunctioning systems, apparatus, or components, using test equipment and hand tools to locate the cause of a breakdown and correct the problem;

Perform business management duties, such as maintaining records or files, preparing reports, or ordering supplies or equipment; maintain current electrician's license or identification card to meet governmental regulations; direct or train workers to install, maintain, or repair electrical wiring, equipment, or fixtures;

Test electrical systems or continuity of circuits in electrical wiring, equipment, or fixtures, using testing devices, such as ohmmeters, voltmeters, or oscilloscopes, to ensure compatibility and safety of system; advise management on whether continued operation of equipment could be hazardous; clear machine jams, using hand tools;

Monitor sawing machines, adjusting speed and tension and clearing jams to ensure proper operation; set up, operate, or tend saws or machines that cut or trim wood to specified dimensions, such as circular saws, band saws, or multiple-blade sawing machines; inspect stock for imperfections or to estimate grades or qualities of stock or workpieces;
Measure and mark cutting lines on materials, using a ruler, pencil, chalk, and marking gauge; shape or cut materials to specified measurements, using hand tools, machines, or power saws;

Extract and pack the final product, including inspecting and removing defective products and packaging material; secure finished packaged items by hand tying, sewing, gluing, stapling, or attaching fasteners;
Operate auxiliary machines and equipment, such as grinders, to prepare or further process products; perform minor repairs and clean machinery and equipment;
Follow established safety rules and regulations and maintain a safe and clean environment;

Remove finished packaged items from the machine and separate rejected items. Count and record finished and rejected packaged items. Clean and sterilize vats and factory processing areas. Record production and test data for each food product batch, such as the ingredients used, temperature, and time cycle. Monitor and inspect equipment and report malfunctions or undesirable tastes to supervisors. Provide assistance to plumbers in repairing or replacing water, sewer, or waste lines, and in daily maintenance activities. Test electrical systems to determine voltages, using voltage meters. Inspect, measure, or test completed products to ensure conformance to specifications. Monitor the fitting, burning, and welding processes to avoid overheating of parts or warping, shrinking, distortion, or expansion of material. Detect faulty operation of equipment or defective materials and notify supervisors. Develop, maintain, and follow operation, safety, and maintenance procedures. Maintain daily logs of operation, maintenance, and safety activities, including test results and instrument readings. Analyze problems and take appropriate action to ensure continuous and reliable operation of equipment and systems.

Tend or operate machines that package products. Adjust machine components and machine tension and pressure according to the size or processing angle of the product. Supply materials to spindles, conveyors, hoppers, or other feeding devices and unload packaged products. Follow recipes to produce food products of specified flavor, texture, clarity, or color. Set up, operate, and tend equipment that cooks, mixes, blends, or processes ingredients in the manufacturing of food products according to formulas or recipes.

Homogenize or pasteurize material to prevent separation or obtain prescribed butterfat content, using a homogenizing device. Mix or blend ingredients according to recipes, using paddles, agitators, or heating vats. Give directions to other workers who assist in the batchmaking process. Determine mixing sequences based on knowledge of temperature effects and the solubility of specific ingredients. Turn valve controls to start equipment and adjust operations to maintain product quality.

Attach identification labels to finished packaged items. Clean and lubricate equipment to maintain efficient operation, using air hoses, cleaning fluids, and hand tools. Observe gauges and thermometers to control the mixing chamber temperature and maintain proper levels. Activate valves to maintain required amounts of water in boilers, to adjust supplies of combustion air, and to control the flow of fuel into burners.

Activate and adjust gas flow and pressure to achieve desired flames. Select and install torches, torch tips, filler rods, and flux according to welding specifications. Set dials and timing controls to regulate electrical current, gas flow pressure, heating or cooling cycles. Assemble, align, and clamp workpieces into holding fixtures to bond, heat-treat, or solder fabricated components. Read blueprints or specifications to determine required repairs or fabrication methods.

Perform maintenance and repairs on machinery, including overhauls, and replacement of defective parts, valves, gaskets, or bearings. Monitor boiler water, chemical, and fuel levels, making adjustments to maintain required levels. Inspect equipment for quality assurance and perform necessary adjustments to ensure functionality.

Operate, monitor, and make adjustments to stationary engines, boilers, and auxiliary equipment necessary to supply and maintain steam or heat. Clean and lubricate boilers and auxiliary equipment, making minor adjustments as needed. Check air quality of ventilation systems and make adjustments as needed to comply with safety codes.

Conduct trial runs before welding or soldering, and adjust equipment as necessary. Prepare surfaces for welding, ensuring no loose or foreign matter is present. Identify workpieces, mark weld points, and ensure conformity to specifications. Repair products by dismantling, straightening, reshaping, and reassembling parts using various tools.

Disassemble equipment to gain access to defects and perform necessary repairs. Analyze engineering drawings, blueprints, and specifications to plan layouts, assembly, and operations. Record operational information on specified production reports to track performance and compliance.

Mark or tag materials with job numbers and identifying marks as required. Fabricate parts for construction, repair, or assembly using appropriate tools and adhering to safety protocols. Develop templates and models for projects based on calculations derived from specifications.

Train or supervise other workers assisting in operations as needed. Order necessary supplies for repairs and maintenance. Monitor production schedules to ensure timely completion of tasks and meet quality assurance standards.
